title: Lord of the Rings The Fellowship of the Ring
author: J R R Tolkien
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "Lord of the Rings The Fellowship of the Ring" book by J R R Tolkien?
Options:
A. On the far side of the room by the outer door, a woman sat in a chair. Flowing down around her shoulders was long, yellow hair; she was dressed in a green gown, green like young reeds, shot through with silver similar to glistening drops of dew; her belt was gold, shaped like a chain of flag-lilies and set with forget-me-nots of a pale blue. Surrounding her feet in large vessels of green and brown earthenware, white water lilies floated, making it seem as if she were seated on a throne in the middle of a pool.
B. In a chair, at the far side of the room facing the outer door, sat a woman. Her long yellow hair rippled down her shoulders; her gown was green, green as young reeds, shot with silver like beads of dew; and her belt was of gold, shaped like a chain of flag-lilies set with the pale-blue eyes of forget-me-nots. About her feet in wide vessels of green and brown earthenware, white water-lilies were floating, so that she seemed to be enthroned in the midst of a pool.
C. Across the room next to the outer door, a woman was seated in a chair. Her long yellow hair cascaded over her shoulders; she was clothed in a green gown, green as young reeds, accented with silver reminiscent of beads of dew; her belt was golden, formed like a chain of flag-lilies and inset with pale blue forget-me-nots. At her feet in wide containers of green and brown clay, white water lilies floated, giving the impression that she was enthroned amidst a pool.
D. Seated in a chair on the opposite side of the room near the outer door was a woman. Her long blonde hair cascaded down over her shoulders; she wore a green gown, the color of young reeds, accented with silver reminiscent of dewdrops; her belt was gold, formed in the shape of a chain of flag-lilies and studded with pale-blue forget-me-nots. At her feet in wide containers made of green and brown clay, white water lilies floated, giving the impression she was enthroned in the center of a pond.  
Answer:

options.A: On the far side of the room by the outer door, a woman sat in a chair. Flowing down around her shoulders was long, yellow hair; she was dressed in a green gown, green like young reeds, shot through with silver similar to glistening drops of dew; her belt was gold, shaped like a chain of flag-lilies and set with forget-me-nots of a pale blue. Surrounding her feet in large vessels of green and brown earthenware, white water lilies floated, making it seem as if she were seated on a throne in the middle of a pool.
options.B: In a chair, at the far side of the room facing the outer door, sat a woman. Her long yellow hair rippled down her shoulders; her gown was green, green as young reeds, shot with silver like beads of dew; and her belt was of gold, shaped like a chain of flag-lilies set with the pale-blue eyes of forget-me-nots. About her feet in wide vessels of green and brown earthenware, white water-lilies were floating, so that she seemed to be enthroned in the midst of a pool.
options.C: Across the room next to the outer door, a woman was seated in a chair. Her long yellow hair cascaded over her shoulders; she was clothed in a green gown, green as young reeds, accented with silver reminiscent of beads of dew; her belt was golden, formed like a chain of flag-lilies and inset with pale blue forget-me-nots. At her feet in wide containers of green and brown clay, white water lilies floated, giving the impression that she was enthroned amidst a pool.
options.D: Seated in a chair on the opposite side of the room near the outer door was a woman. Her long blonde hair cascaded down over her shoulders; she wore a green gown, the color of young reeds, accented with silver reminiscent of dewdrops; her belt was gold, formed in the shape of a chain of flag-lilies and studded with pale-blue forget-me-nots. At her feet in wide containers made of green and brown clay, white water lilies floated, giving the impression she was enthroned in the center of a pond.
